  /**
   * INI related APIs
   */
  namespace INI {
    /**
     * Parse an INI string into a JavaScript object.
     *
     * Supports sections (`[section]` becomes a nested object), key-value pairs
     * with optional whitespace around `=`, and comment lines starting with `;`
     * or `#`. Values of `true`, `false`, and `null` are coerced to their
     * JavaScript equivalents; numeric strings are coerced to numbers; everything
     * else is returned as a string. Quoted values (single or double) have their
     * surrounding quotes stripped.
     *
     * @category Utilities
     *
     * @param input The INI string to parse
     * @returns A JavaScript object representing the parsed INI data
     *
     * @example
     * ```ts
     * import { INI } from "bun";
     *
     * const result = INI.parse(`
     * ; This is a comment
     * name = my-app
     * version = 1.0.0
     * debug = true
     *
     * [database]
     * host = localhost
     * port = 5432
     * `);
     * // {
     * //   name: "my-app",
     * //   version: "1.0.0",
     * //   debug: true,
     * //   database: { host: "localhost", port: 5432 }
     * // }
     * ```
     */
    export function parse(input: string): Record<string, any>;

    /**
     * Convert a JavaScript object into an INI-formatted string.
     *
     * Top-level scalar values are written as `key = value` lines. Top-level
     * objects are written as `[section]` headers followed by their key-value
     * pairs. Deeply nested objects (within a section) are skipped. Arrays,
     * booleans, numbers, and `null` are serialized to their string equivalents.
     *
     * @category Utilities
     *
     * @param input The JavaScript object to stringify
     * @returns An INI-formatted string
     *
     * @example
     * ```ts
     * import { INI } from "bun";
     *
     * console.log(INI.stringify({
     *   name: "my-app",
     *   debug: true,
     *   database: {
     *     host: "localhost",
     *     port: 5432,
     *   },
     * }));
     * // name = my-app
     * // debug = true
     * //
     * // [database]
     * // host = localhost
     * // port = 5432
     * ```
     */
    export function stringify(input: Record<string, any>): string;
  }
